messages.content:
A solar energy company learns of several recent cyber-attacks targeting other companies in their industry and realizes they could be next. The company initiates an analysis to weigh the measures needed to counter the potential threat and to minimize the impact to business.

Which security principle is illustrated in this example?

Compliance

Accessibility

Governance

Risk Management

I don't know this yet.
Risk Management

What is "defense in depth"?

when security is an ongoing process rather than a one-and-done

security defense that is confidential, integrous, and available

security designed with multiple levels of fail-safe practices

when security is part of the design process and built in from the start

I don't know this yet.
security designed with multiple levels of fail-safe practices

Which statement describes one action that employees can take to help strengthen an organization’s defenses?

Report phishing attempts promptly upon discovery.

Contact security before updating software applications.

Use VPN when devices are left unattended.

Use similar passwords across systems.

I don't know this yet.
Report phishing attempts promptly upon discovery.

Accenture is working with a client to improve their current security infrastructure. The client wants to redefine the security programs, create long-term plans for effective audits, and proactively plan against future threats.

What might Accenture recommend to this client?

Create an improved operational technical security practice that leverages new and improved practices.

Use a complete offshore security practice of security experts to monitor security events.

Develop a long-term security strategy which includes a risk management plan.

Create a cybersecurity team of internal, client team experts who partner with external experts, whose focus is strictly internal audits.

I don't know this yet.
Develop a long-term security strategy which includes a risk management plan.

What is the best-case scenario for employees to ensure the security standards in their virtual workspaces are the same as the physical office?

Use their enterprise password for their home devices for consistency with their work devices.

In a virtual environment such as a home, it’s OK to leave the devices unlocked.

Use only company approved devices and software for business and client work.

Configure company applications to require single-factor authentication.

I don't know this yet.
Use only company approved devices and software for business and client work.

In addition to innovation, which strength can Accenture apply to help a global telecom solve security problems?

governance and tenancy knowledge

global experience and industry expertise

cadence expertise and closing experience

legitimacy and commercial experience

I don't know this yet.
global experience and industry expertise

Accenture’s Security practice makes use of a number of accelerators when building solutions for our clients.

What is the purpose of these accelerators?

to speed up connection times while accessing secure online applications

to reduce the turnaround time for investigating potential security incidents

to decrease the length of the contracting process when selling to new security clients

to deploy and integrate security features in a shorter period of time

I don't know this yet.
to deploy and integrate security features in a shorter period of time

An international pharmaceutical company is fully compliant with local and international regulations. However, they suffered a major data breach that exploited a six-month-old vulnerability. The CIO asked Accenture "How can this be possible?"

How should Accenture respond?

Because compliance to local and international laws does not necessarily enforce security.

Because the Security Operations team does not handle compliance issues.

Because the security policy documentation was not kept up to date.

Because the local and international compliance laws conflict.

I don't know this yet.
Because compliance to local and international laws does not necessarily enforce security.

A Product Manager has been given responsibility for overseeing the development of a new software application that will be deployed to a group of Accenture clients.

What would be the most time-saving and cost-effective way for the Product Manager to address the new application’s security considerations?

Utilize a DevSecOps approach to incorporate security into the development process from the beginning.

Contract with an external vendor to develop a security solution separately from the main application.

Schedule development of security features after the application’s initial release.

Design the application’s security features after the application’s initial build is complete.

I don't know this yet.
Utilize a DevSecOps approach to incorporate security into the development process from the beginning.

Which Accenture team builds custom code in a secure-by-design way, to be monitored and kept secure for an entire lifecycle?

Penetration Testing

Security Operations

Social Engineering

DevSecOps

I don't know this yet.
DevSecOps

A company employs numerous layers of security checkpoints throughout its technology infrastructure as a security strategy.

What is this strategy called?

defense in depth

penetration testing

principle of least privilege

compliance assessment

I don't know this yet.
defense in depth

What is Accenture’s approach when it comes to helping our clients with security?

Address the client’s security risks only after they arrive. A data breach opens all doors on selling new security deals.

Help the client create a secure, in-house data center, that follows international regulations such as ISO 27001.

Create a universal security solution to fit the needs of all clients. Accenture's unique platform can be embedded in any client architecture.

Embed security in all aspects of the client work, solving even the most complex of the client’s cyber challenges.

I don't know this yet.
Embed security in all aspects of the client work, solving even the most complex of the client’s cyber challenges.

A real estate company wants to reduce its workload and allow prospective buyers to view properties from home.

Which technology can be used to create a walkthrough that immerses the buyer into the home space?

Simulated Reality

Virtual Reality

Mixed Reality

Augmented Reality

I don't know this yet.
Virtual Reality

A mobile phone app asks the user to point the phone's camera at a room and then applies the phone's LIDAR scanning function.

What does this technology do?

optimizes the frame rate of the phone's display based on the strength of the network

records a video of the room and automatically adjusts the brightness and color balance

scans the room to locate and connect to other Extended Reality enabled devices

measures distances between objects to create a virtual representation of the room

I don't know this yet.
measures distances between objects to create a virtual representation of the room

When it comes to Extended Reality (XR) in business, what fact differentiates Accenture from other companies?

Accenture and Microsoft are currently the only two companies who can provide and support a holistic XR service.

Accenture has designed its own exclusive Virtual Reality headset to share with our diamond clients.

Accenture has acquired Microsoft's XR division to create a new platform which will change the way businesses interact.

Accenture offers a one-size-fits-all set of XR services to all clients regardless of industry.

I don't know this yet.

I don't have access to real-time information on specific partnerships or exclusive offerings by companies like Accenture, so the second, third, and fourth options are not accurate. Accenture might differentiate from other companies by providing comprehensive XR solutions tailored to meet the unique needs of businesses in various industries, offering a wide range of services and expertise in implementing XR technologies effectively.

A utility company hires Accenture to improve training and experiences for utility workers.

What is one way Accenture can use Virtual Reality as a solution?

Use improved data collection to ensure that safety measure are being met.

Provide real-time monitoring of workers while at dangerous sites.

Develop immersive training on how to conduct power line inspections.

Demonstrate how technology can be used for improved energy efficiency.

I don't know this yet.
Develop immersive training on how to conduct power line inspections.

What is the vibrating or buzzing of a hand controller known as?

spatial mapping

foveated rendering

lidar scanning

haptic feedback

I don't know this yet.
haptic feedback

What is a defining feature of the Metaverse?

It will allow users to cross over from one experience to another.

It will enable users to bypass security controls across virtual spaces.

It will allow Virtual Reality worlds to be more easily accessed with a mobile device.

It will provide better spatial mapping for gaming experiences.

I don't know this yet.
It will allow users to cross over from one experience to another.

What is the screen door effect?

when a broader viewing range results in a more detailed image

when the boundaries of the user's peripheral vision expand

when a low-resolution display creates a pixelated image

when high latency causes motion sickness in the viewer

I don't know this yet.
when a low-resolution display creates a pixelated image

How is a game like Pokemon Go an example of Augmented Reality?

The digital images completely replace the real world.

The digital environment is made to look very realistic.

The user overlays digital content on the real world.

The user must have wearable technology to play.

I don't know this yet.
The user overlays digital content on the real world.

What are the most important benefits of using Virtual Reality in business training?

high video resolutions and ultra-realistic graphics

collecting users' personal data for marketing purposes

increased memory retention and decreased cost

compliance with travel and security regulations

I don't know this yet.
increased memory retention and decreased cost

Why has Extended Reality (XR) recently become more common in the business world?

The technology to create large-scale XR experiences was less affordable before now.

Individual users have much more money to spend on personal XR devices than before.

Government regulations prohibited use of XR technology until just the last few years.

Employees were not interested in using XR for their work activities until recently.

I don't know this yet.
The technology to create large-scale XR experiences was less affordable before now.

What is a potential benefit of applying Extended Reality solutions to surgical science?

It allows the visualization of vital information in real time during surgical procedures.

It monitors hygiene by tracking contact between patients and hospital staff.

It estimates the anesthesia required by calculating a patient's body mass index (BMI).

It predicts the amount of healing time needed based on medication and scans.

I don't know this yet.
It allows the visualization of vital information in real time during surgical procedures.
If you have any more questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask!

How could the manufacturing sector utilize Extended Reality?

Streamline efficiencies by reducing workforce numbers.

Create prototypes of new products without wasting resources.

Allow consumers to buy a product before its market launch.

Predict the operating costs and resource needs of upcoming work.

I don't know this yet.
Create prototypes of new products without wasting resources.

Which part of the drug discovery life cycle can quantum computing impact the most?

laboratory testing

molecular simulation

clinical trials

regulatory approval

I don't know this yet.
molecular simulation

What does it mean when we say that we are currently in the Noisy, Intermediate-Scale Quantum Computing (NISQ) stage?

Quantum computers are still in the early theoretical stage, with no actual machines built yet.

Quantum computers are able to solve a number of current problems, and will continue to advance.

Some quantum computers can now be purchased cheaply in stores by individual consumers.

Quantum computers have already reached peak performance and cannot be improved further.

I don't know this yet.
Quantum computers are able to solve a number of current problems, and will continue to advance.

A trucking company is expanding its business and recently added 50 new trucks to its fleet delivering to numerous locations. The company is facing some challenges managing the new additions. Which of the company's problems could be solved more easily using quantum computing?

routing the trucks effectively to optimize fuel consumption

collecting travel data from all the company's trucks in real-time

automating the inventory operations to handle the expansion

tracking to ensure that shipments are sent and received on time

I don't know this yet.
routing the trucks effectively to optimize fuel consumption

What is a barrier to entry preventing quantum computing hardware from becoming more widely available?

They cost companies millions of dollars to manufacture.

They need to be kept at a constant temperature of 0°C.

They are not expected to save data for more than a year.

They are limited to solving only intractable problems.

I don't know this yet.
They cost companies millions of dollars to manufacture.

What is a current concern regarding the advancement of quantum computing?

Computers will replace humans in all decision-making tasks.

Algorithmic trading may cause stock market instability.

Steering qubits towards desired states will introduce bias.

Existing cryptography may be easily cracked.

I don't know this yet.
Existing cryptography may be easily cracked.

What does the term "classical computer" refer to?

a computer that is not connected to the internet

an early computer built before the 1950s

a computer that operates using conventional bits

a computer modified to perform quantum functions

I don't know this yet.
a computer that operates using conventional bits

What is meant by "applied quantum computing?"

processing simple mathematical functions more quickly

installing quantum computers directly in company offices

using quantum computers to solve real business problems

building more advanced quantum computing hardware

I don't know this yet.
using quantum computers to solve real business problems

Why is quantum computing useful for optimization problems?

It evaluates solutions one by one in a sequential manner.

It divides the intractable complexity into bits to calculate simple solutions.

It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.

It uses human intuition to only explore certain potential solutions.

I don't know this yet.
It can perform operations on a combination of all possible solutions.

Which term refers to the strong information correlation between qubits?

quantum annealing

entanglement

linear combination

coherence

I don't know this yet.
entanglement

What is a use case of factorization in quantum computing?

code decryption

optimization of routes

randomizing data sets

simulating language patterns

I don't know this yet.
code decryption

Which technology is making quantum computing easier to access and adopt?

Virtual Reality

Cloud

Blockchain

Edge Computing

I don't know this yet.
Cloud
